notes: Direction B: dropped the deep stack for a single Hebbian patch-bank layer (H=8192, K=16 byte one-hot patches => D_in=4096), SoftHebb-Oja soft-WTA rule with tau=0.5, eta=0.01 annealed to 0, batch 32 x SEQ_LEN 4096, stride-8 patch subsampling, 200M training chars per spec. The spec's wall-time estimate (~120 s Hebbian + ~35 s control + ~20 s ridge + ~20 s overhead = ~210 s) was optimistic on the Modal A100-SXM4-80GB: the Hebbian sweep alone took 272.5 s (1525 steps at ~178 ms/step including y@W residual plus dW = y^T @ residual matmuls at H=8192, D_in=4096, N_rows=16352). Ridge collection ran only 24 s before the 300 s SIGALRM hit; the Cholesky solve and both variants' quick_eval never executed. Per-channel mean-activation entropy stayed pinned at log(H)=9.011 throughout (max-entropy), the same pathology as pass 1: SoftHebb soft-WTA on one-hot byte patches does not break symmetry — every channel sees roughly the same input distribution and the Oja-residual update averages out. No tau bump fired (entropy did not drop below 0.3*log(H)). Disqualified for wall-clock; neither Hebbian nor random-projection control produced a val_char_acc measurement. Per /survey policy on Exit-2 outcomes, did not retry. Confirmed finding: the H=8192 Hebbian sweep is not feasible within 300 s with this matmul shape; even if it had finished, the entropy trace indicates channels would not have differentiated, so the spec's primary hypothesis (positive Hebbian-vs-random delta) is implicitly negative on byte streams.
